title: AFS Atlas
summary: Interactive guide to the AFS session harness, background agents, and editor integration surfaces.

tutorials.title: Session Harness and Prompt Loop
tutorials.summary: Follow how AFS prepares client payloads, records session activity, and composes model-facing prompts.

steps.title: Prepare-client CLI
steps.summary: The main CLI entrypoint for building client payloads, packs, and prompt artifacts.

steps.title: Session event CLI
steps.summary: This is where live prompt, turn, and task lifecycle events are recorded.

steps.title: Stable activity contract
steps.summary: The harness declares the supported session activity events here.

steps.title: Live session activity updates
steps.summary: This function mutates the live client payload as prompt, turn, and task events arrive.

steps.title: Dynamic system prompt builder
steps.summary: AFS composes system prompts from base instructions plus the current session context here.

tutorials.title: Background Agents and Replay
tutorials.summary: Use this path to understand how AFS tracks background agents and reconstructs session history.

steps.title: Agent process status
steps.summary: The CLI view of running, failed, and stopped background agents starts here.

steps.title: Session replay
steps.summary: The replay command bridges the CLI to the event timeline builder.

steps.title: Event timeline builder
steps.summary: This collects and summarizes chronological session events.

steps.title: Health snapshot
steps.summary: The AFS health summary aggregates context, MCP, maintenance, and supervisor state.

tutorials.title: Editor and Wrapper Surfaces
tutorials.summary: This walkthrough covers the wrapper and editor integrations that consume the session harness.

steps.title: Native client wrapper
steps.summary: The shell wrapper launches session hooks and emits prompt and turn lifecycle events.

steps.title: Emacs helper dispatch
steps.summary: The Emacs command palette for AFS sessions, replay, and agent views starts here.

steps.title: Emacs prepare-client view
steps.summary: This command renders prepared client payloads and prompt artifacts inside Emacs.

steps.title: VS Code turn lifecycle
steps.summary: The VS Code CLI transport begins tracked turns here and threads task events through them.
